title: Bridge Essay: Mind wandering perspective on attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder.
concept: Spontaneous thought — mind-wandering, default mode network, creative cognition, involuntary thought.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Non-fabrication** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Spontaneous thought — mind-wandering, default mode network, creative cognition, involuntary thought.

**Mind wandering perspective on attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder.** - Neurosci Biobehav Rev (2018). DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2018.07.010. PMID: 30036553. Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) is a common neurodevelopmental disorder associated with a range of mental health, neurocognitive and functional problems. Although the diagnosis is based on descriptions of behaviour, individuals with ADHD characteristically describe excessive spontaneous mind wandering (MW). MW in individuals with ADHD reflects constant mental activity which lacks topic stability and content consistency. Based on this review of the neural correlates of ADHD and MW
